compiled_state.title: RED: Tests for value/effort scorer integration in brainstorm and roadmap
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

Create tests/skills/test-value-effort-scorer-integration.sh with 6 behavioral tests:
- Test 1: Scorer prompt file exists at plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/value-effort-scorer.md and is non-empty
- Test 2: Scorer prompt contains parseable priority matrix covering boundary conditions: (5,1)→P0/P1, (1,5)→P3/P4, (5,5)→P1/P2, (1,1)→P3/P4
- Test 3: Brainstorm SKILL.md references shared/prompts/value-effort-scorer.md AND ticket create uses -p with a variable
- Test 4: Roadmap SKILL.md references shared/prompts/value-effort-scorer.md AND uses 1-5 scale (not 1-10)
- Test 5: Scorer defines value scale (1-5) and effort scale (1-5) with 5 distinct levels each
- Test 6: Roadmap Phase 3 example output and Enabler Logic examples all use 1-5 scale values

Add .test-index entry with RED marker.
Tests must FAIL (RED) before implementation.

## File Impact
- CREATE: tests/skills/test-value-effort-scorer-integration.sh
- MODIFY: .test-index

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] `ruff check` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/*.py $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/**/*.py
- [ ] `ruff format --check` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff format --check $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/*.py $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/**/*.py
- [ ] 6 test functions added
  Verify: grep -c '^test_' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-value-effort-scorer-integration.sh
- [ ] New tests FAIL (RED) before implementation
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-value-effort-scorer-integration.sh 2>&1 | tail -3
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Gap Analysis Amendment (major)
Tests must cover both consumer contexts:
- Tests 1-2, 5: Scorer prompt validation (generic — applies to both consumers)
- Test 3: Brainstorm-specific integration (scorer reference + -p variable in create)
- Test 4, 6: Roadmap-specific integration (scorer reference + 1-5 scale + example values)
The scorer is a prompt template, not executable code — both consumers invoke it identically by reading the prompt. The test file does NOT need separate consumer-context scenarios because the scorer has no code path differences.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: RED tests created for value/effort scorer integration
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
